00951| Preludes
00952| --------
00953| 
00954| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00955| 
00956| :dp:`fls_po4gw6t2ptwu`
00957| A :dt:`prelude` is a collection of :t:`entities <entity>` that are automatically
00958| brought :t:`in scope` of every :t:`module` in a :t:`crate`.
00959| 
00960| :dp:`fls_1fSWTLr3AP5z`
00961| A :dt:`prelude entity` is an :t:`entity` declared in a :t:`prelude`.
00962| 
00963| :dp:`fls_eS8zAvhDy3gJ`
00964| A :dt:`prelude name` is a :t:`name` of a :t:`prelude entity`.
00965| 
00966| :dp:`fls_n4102qskkmz2`
00967| The :dt:`core prelude` is a :t:`prelude` that brings :t:`in scope` of every
00968| :t:`module` all re-exported :t:`entities <entity>` from the
00969| :std:`core::prelude::rust_2021` :t:`module`.
00970| 
00971| :dp:`fls_atvnwly4w8g2`
00972| An :dt:`external prelude` is a :t:`prelude` that brings :t:`in scope` of the
00973| :dt:`crate root module` the :t:`entities <entity>` of the :t:`[crate]s` imported
00974| using external :dt:`[crate import]s` or supplied by a tool. If the external
00975| :t:`crate import` uses a :t:`renaming`, then the :t:`identifier` of the
00976| :t:`renaming` is instead added to the :t:`external prelude`. The core
00977| :t:`crate` is always added to the :t:`external prelude` unless the :t:`crate
00978| root` is subject to :t:`attribute` ``no_core``.
00979| 
00980| :dp:`fls_pbc7ktlu0pl`
00981| The :dt:`language prelude` is a :t:`prelude` that brings :t:`in scope` of every
00982| :t:`module` the following :t:`entities <entity>`:
00983| 
00984| * :dp:`fls_frjv68kqqxfh`
00985|   Boolean type :c:`bool`.
00986| 
00987| * :dp:`fls_rf6a2ae3y7vu`
00988|   :t:`[Built-in attribute]s`.
00989| 
00990| * :dp:`fls_sxnnkzmuvexa`
00991|   :t:`[Floating-point type]s` :c:`f32` and :c:`f64`.
00992| 
00993| * :dp:`fls_qsyorqjkdh2t`
00994|   :t:`[Integer type]s` :c:`i8`, :c:`i16`, :c:`i32`, :c:`i64`, :c:`i128`,
00995|   :c:`isize`, :c:`u8`, :c:`u16`, :c:`u32`, :c:`u64`, :c:`u128`, and :c:`usize`.
00996| 
00997| * :dp:`fls_aolj6abvp9sa`
00998|   :t:`[Textual type]s` :c:`char` and :c:`str`.
00999| 
01000| :dp:`fls_of4n3vv15l5z`
01001| The :dt:`macro_use prelude` is a :t:`prelude` that brings :t:`in scope` of the
01002| :t:`crate root module` the :t:`entities <entity>` of :t:`[macro]s` from
01003| external :t:`[crate]s` that were imported using an external :t:`crate import`.
01004|

01007| Use Imports
01008| -----------
01009| 
01010| .. rubric:: Syntax
01011| 
01012| .. syntax::
01013| 
01014|    UseImport ::=
01015|        $$use$$ UseImportContent $$;$$
01016| 
01017|    UseImportContent ::=
01018|        GlobImport
01019|      | NestingImport
01020|      | SimpleImport
01021| 
01022|    GlobImport ::=
01023|        SimplePathPrefix? $$*$$
01024| 
01025|    NestingImport ::=
01026|        SimplePathPrefix? $${$$ UseImportContentList? $$}$$
01027| 
01028|    SimpleImport ::=
01029|        SimplePath Renaming?
01030| 
01031|    SimplePathPrefix ::=
01032|        SimplePath? $$::$$
01033| 
01034|    UseImportContentList ::=
01035|        UseImportContent ($$,$$ UseImportContent)* $$,$$?
01036| 
01037| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01038| 
01039| :dp:`fls_lyw4t098sxrj`
01040| A :dt:`use import` brings :t:`entities <entity>` :t:`in scope` within the
01041| :t:`scope` of the :t:`block expression` of an :t:`expression-with-block` or
01042| :t:`module` where the
01043| :t:`use import` resides.
01044| 
01045| :dp:`fls_sxo1jb25pl8a`
01046| A :t:`simple path prefix` is the leading :t:`simple path` of a :t:`glob import`
01047| or a :t:`nesting import`.
01048| 
01049| :dp:`fls_WAA4WmohGu6T`
01050| An :dt:`import path prefix` is the fully constructed :t:`path` prefix of a
01051| :t:`use import`. An :t:`import path prefix` for a given
01052| :t:`simple import` or :t:`glob import` is constructed as follows:
01053| 
01054| #. :dp:`fls_IPYvldMqduf4`
01055|    Start the :t:`import path prefix` as follows:
01056| 
01057|    * :dp:`fls_MOXId37fcNPY`
01058|      If the :t:`use import` is a :t:`simple import` then start with the
01059|      :t:`[path segment]s` of the :t:`simple import`'s :t:`simple path`
01060|      :t:`path prefix`.
01061| 
01062|    * :dp:`fls_2UyFcB6Our1v`
01063|      If the :t:`use import` is a :t:`glob import` then start with the
01064|      :t:`[path segment]s` of the :t:`glob import`'s :t:`simple path prefix`.
01065| 
01066|    * :dp:`fls_irdKqoYzBM0M`
01067|      If the :t:`use import` is a :t:`nesting import` then start with the
01068|      :t:`[path segment]s` of the :t:`nesting import`'s :t:`simple path prefix`.
01069| 
01070| #. :dp:`fls_gAWsqibl4GLq`
01071|    Then if the current :t:`use import` is the child of a :t:`nesting import`,
01072|    prepend the :t:`nesting import`'s :t:`simple path prefix` to the
01073|    :t:`import path prefix`. Repeat this step with the :t:`nesting import` as
01074|    the current :t:`use import`.
01075| 
01076| :dp:`fls_2bkcn83smy2y`
01077| A :dt:`simple import` is a :t:`use import` that brings all :t:`entities <entity>`
01078| it refers to into scope, optionally with a different
01079| :t:`name` than they are declared with by using a :dt:`renaming`.
01080| 
01081| :dp:`fls_v3a6y2ze44v2`
01082| A :dt:`glob import` is a :t:`use import` that brings all :t:`entities <entity>`
01083| exported by the :t:`module` or :t:`enum` its :t:`import path prefix` resolves to
01084| into :t:`scope`.
01085| 
01086| :dp:`fls_JHU0ersYB6eL`
01087| An :t:`import path prefix` shall resolve to a :t:`module` or :t:`enum`.
01088| 
01089| :dp:`fls_jlNKxkuhsvX4`
01090| A :t:`glob import` brings :t:`[name]s` into :t:`scope` as follows:
01091| 
01092| * :dp:`fls_q0KFfNALMslq`
01093|   If the :t:`import path prefix` resolves to a :t:`module`, bring all
01094|   :t:`[name]s` in the :t:`module` that are visible from the location of the
01095|   :t:`glob import` into :t:`scope`.
01096| 
01097| * :dp:`fls_Hy17LzOFGfOp`
01098|   If the :t:`import path prefix` resolves to an :t:`enum`, bring the
01099|   :t:`[name]s` of all of the :t:`enum`'s :t:`[enum variant]s` into :t:`scope`.
01100| 
01101| :dp:`fls_90hQvSh7Bfyg`
01102| A :dt:`simple import path` is the :t:`path` constructed by appending the last
01103| :t:`path segment` of a :t:`simple import`'s :t:`simple path` to the
01104| :t:`import path prefix`.
01105| 
01106| :dp:`fls_wRmvtgQkFA6w`
01107| A :t:`simple import` brings :t:`[name]s` into :t:`scope` as follows:
01108| 
01109| * :dp:`fls_kz2Gij5wHXnl`
01110|   If the :t:`simple path` is keyword ``self`` and:
01111| 
01112|   * :dp:`fls_yY58pFpkig9o`
01113|     The :t:`simple import` is in a :t:`nesting import`, then bring the
01114|     :t:`entity` in :t:`type namespace` that the :t:`import path prefix` resolves
01115|     to into :t:`scope`.
01116| 
01117|   * :dp:`fls_ar03D5rxjzy0`
01118|     Otherwise bring the containing :t:`module` into :t:`scope`.
01119| 
01120| * :dp:`fls_ce73bg0BqV1X`
01121|   Otherwise bring all :t:`entities <entity>` that the :t:`simple import path`
01122|   resolves to that are visible from the location of the
01123|   :t:`simple import` into :t:`scope`.
01124| 
01125| :dp:`fls_FILuR3pfwjw3`
01126| An :t:`Entity` imported by a :t:`simple import` subject to a
01127| :t:`renaming` with :t:`identifier` is brought into :t:`scope` under the
01128| :t:`name` declared by the :t:`renaming`.
01129| 
01130| :dp:`fls_iQOgxNihUEr7`
01131| A :t:`trait` imported by a :t:`simple import` subject to a
01132| :t:`renaming` with character underscore ``_`` is added into :t:`scope` without
01133| a :t:`name`.
01134| 
01135| :dp:`fls_ldr7tsuqw34s`
01136| A :dt:`nesting import` is a :t:`use import` that provides a common
01137| :t:`simple path prefix` for its nested :t:`[use import]s`.
01138| 
01139| :dp:`fls_iNUBX5fJAI1N`
01140| A :t:`glob import` outside of a :t:`nesting import` without a :t:`simple path
01141| prefix` is rejected, but may still be consumed by :t:`[macro]s`.
01142| 
01143| :dp:`fls_RUiFQ17bmRLt`
01144| A :t:`simple import` with a :t:`simple path` with a single :t:`path segment` of
01145| keyword ``self`` shall be subject to the following:
01146| 
01147| * :dp:`fls_hv3xT2CjZuxc`
01148|   It shall either appear in a :t:`nesting import` with a non-empty
01149|   :t:`import path prefix`, or
01150| 
01151| * :dp:`fls_Pxc0Ts8Y7pfW`
01152|   It shall be subject to a :t:`renaming`.
01153| 
01154| :dp:`fls_wB3fVglLOqbZ`
01155| It is a static error if two :t:`[glob import]s` import the same :t:`name` in the
01156| same :t:`namespace` but refer to different :t:`entities <entity>` if the
01157| :t:`name` is used or :t:`shadowed <shadowing>`.
01158| 
01159| :dp:`fls_zmYSBW995kSN`
01160| If two :t:`[glob import]s` import the same :t:`entity` under the same :t:`name`,
01161| the :t:`visibility` of the :t:`name` is the most permissive one.
01162| 
01163| .. rubric:: Examples
01164| 
01165| :dp:`fls_5dlnffim6fso`
01166| The following is a glob import. See :p:`fls_jdknpu3kf865`
01167| for the declaration of modules and functions. The imported functions
01168| are ``create_visible_function``, ``outer_module_visible_function``,
01169| ``visible_function``.
01170| 
01171| .. code-block:: rust
01172| 
01173|    use outer_module::inner_module::*;
01174| 
01175| :dp:`fls_9rhflreuubhq`
01176| The following is a renaming import. The imported function is
01177| ``visible_function`` under the name ``f``.
01178| 
01179| .. code-block:: rust
01180| 
01181|    use outer_module::inner_module::visible_function as f;
01182| 
01183| :dp:`fls_s86dgrdpl1w4`
01184| The following is a selective import. The imported functions are
01185| ``crate_visible_function`` and ``visible_function``.
01186| 
01187| .. code-block:: rust
01188| 
01189|    use outer_module::inner_module
01190|        {crate_visible_function, visible_function}
01191| 
01192| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01193| 
01194| .. _fls_ydmnb7qnmzzq:
01195| 
01196| Shadowing
01197| ---------
01198| 
01199| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01200| 
01201| :dp:`fls_ob0riinmitkl`
01202| :t:`Shadowing` is a property of :t:`[name]s`. A :t:`name` is said to be
01203| :t:`shadowed` when another :t:`name` with the same characters is introduced
01204| in the same :t:`scope` within the same :t:`namespace`, effectively hiding it.
01205| A :t:`name` cannot be referred to by any means once it is :t:`shadowed`.
01206| 
01207| :dp:`fls_fslg89a70e3n`
01208| No :t:`name` shall be :t:`shadowed` except for
01209| 
01210| * :dp:`fls_hp3f4r3399kt`
01211|   :t:`[Prelude name]s`,
01212| 
01213| * :dp:`fls_z8qjpskt13yq`
01214|   The :t:`[name]s` of :t:`[macro]s` within :t:`textual macro scope`,
01215| 
01216| * :dp:`fls_i0gp1y38lr73`
01217|   The :t:`[name]s` of :t:`[variable]s`.
01218| 
01219| :dp:`fls_7pif12rt4s4s`
01220| A :t:`prelude name` shadows other :t:`[prelude name]s` depending on which
01221| :t:`[prelude]s` are included in a :t:`module`. The order of shadowing is as
01222| follows, where a later :t:`prelude name` shadows earlier :t:`prelude name`:
01223| 
01224| #. :dp:`fls_are9qz67p7b6`
01225|    :t:`Language prelude` :t:`names <prelude name>`.
01226| 
01227| #. :dp:`fls_4tis5syofyg0`
01228|    Standard library :t:`prelude` :t:`names <prelude name>`.
01229| 
01230| #. :dp:`fls_u0tsnkhacr06`
01231|    :t:`macro_use prelude` :t:`names <prelude name>`.
01232| 
01233| #. :dp:`fls_iaklf84guczc`
01234|    Tool :t:`prelude` :t:`names <prelude name>`.
01235| 
01236| #. :dp:`fls_a0zovslu2v4u`
01237|    :t:`External prelude` :t:`names <prelude name>`.
01238| 
01239| .. _fls_40xoego2thsp:
01240| 
01241| Resolution
01242| ----------
01243| 
01244| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01245| 
01246| :dp:`fls_ho4kem1slcxg`
01247| :dt:`resolution` is the process of finding a unique interpretation for a
01248| :t:`field access expression`, a :t:`method call expression`, a :t:`call
01249| expression` or a :t:`path`.
01250| 
01251| :dp:`fls_7le2vcdbtxbq`
01252| A :t:`construct` that is being resolved is said to be :dt:`under resolution`.
01253| 
01254| :dp:`fls_x3alg07yd7hx`
01255| A :dt:`dereference type` is either a :t:`reference type` or a :t:`type` that
01256| implements the :std:`core::ops::Deref` :t:`trait`.
01257| 
01258| :dp:`fls_4hulwazdu20i`
01259| A :dt:`dereference type chain` is a sequence of :t:`[dereference type]s`. A
01260| :t:`dereference type chain` starts with an initial :t:`dereference type`. From
01261| then on, the :t:`dereference type chain` continues as follows:
01262| 
01263| * :dp:`fls_ptocwx5p25lj`
01264|   If the previous :t:`dereference type` is a :t:`reference type`, then the
01265|   :t:`dereference type chain` continues with the inner :t:`type` of the
01266|   previous :t:`dereference type`.
01267| 
01268| * :dp:`fls_ygam5nisv98c`
01269|   Otherwise the :t:`dereference type chain` continues with :t:`type`
01270|   :std:`core::ops::Deref::Target` of the previous :t:`dereference type`.
01271| 
01272| .. _fls_xcwfotmq2e5d:
01273| 
01274| Field Resolution
01275| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01276| 
01277| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01278| 
01279| :dp:`fls_1nxknwjdp0am`
01280| :dt:`Field resolution` is a form of :t:`resolution` that applies to a
01281| :t:`field access expression`.
01282| 
01283| :dp:`fls_j1bip4w30q8`
01284| A :dt:`candidate container type` is the :t:`type` of the :dt:`container operand`
01285| of a :t:`field access expression` :t:`under resolution`.
01286| 
01287| :dp:`fls_jrk3gzqvqr8e`
01288| A :dt:`candidate container type chain` is a sequence of
01289| :t:`[candidate container type]s`. The :t:`candidate container type chain`
01290| starts with the :t:`type` of the :t:`container operand` of the
01291| :t:`field access expression` :t:`under resolution`. From then on, the
01292| :t:`candidate container type chain` is treated as a
01293| :t:`dereference type chain`.
01294| 
01295| :dp:`fls_asn20qx16sr6`
01296| A :dt:`candidate field` is a :t:`field` of a :t:`candidate container type`
01297| that is visible from the location of the :t:`field access expression`
01298| :t:`under resolution`.
01299| 
01300| :dp:`fls_yeuayil6uxzx`
01301| A :dt:`field selector` is a :t:`construct` that selects the :t:`field` to be
01302| accessed in a :t:`field access expression`.
01303| 
01304| :dp:`fls_G7nIZNc3Nk4M`
01305| An :dt:`indexed field selector` is a :t:`field selector` where the selected
01306| :t:`field` is indicated by an index.
01307| 
01308| :dp:`fls_NHqdt7U8EoZb`
01309| A :dt:`named field selector` is a :t:`field selector` where the selected
01310| :t:`field` is indicated by an :t:`identifier`.
01311| 
01312| :dp:`fls_jzoon4x89zp7`
01313| A :dt:`candidate indexed field` is a :t:`candidate field` whose position in the
01314| :t:`type` of the :t:`container operand` matches the index of an
01315| :t:`indexed field selector`.
01316| 
01317| :dp:`fls_r80pixfoe5hk`
01318| A :dt:`candidate named field` is a :t:`candidate field` whose :t:`name` matches
01319| the characters of a :t:`named field selector`.
01320| 
01321| :dp:`fls_40oa0j6aiop3`
01322| :t:`Field resolution` of a :t:`field access expression` with an
01323| :t:`indexed field selector` proceeds as follows:
01324| 
01325| #. :dp:`fls_2bp1zs7qaz7o`
01326|    For each :t:`candidate container type` of the
01327|    :t:`candidate container type chain`
01328| 
01329|    #. :dp:`fls_s14fegwhwnc8`
01330|       Try to locate a :t:`candidate indexed field` of the
01331|       :t:`candidate container type`.
01332| 
01333|    #. :dp:`fls_tfjm27ydiake`
01334|       If such a :t:`candidate indexed field` exists and is visible at the point
01335|       of the :t:`field access expression`, then the :t:`field access expression`
01336|       resolves to that :t:`candidate indexed field` and :t:`field resolution`
01337|       stops.
01338| 
01339| :dp:`fls_p6hgoqo0kcx`
01340| :t:`Field resolution` of a :t:`field access expression` with a
01341| :t:`named field selector` proceeds as follows:
01342| 
01343| #. :dp:`fls_e7sj392ohvbd`
01344|    For each :t:`candidate container type` of the
01345|    :t:`candidate container type chain`
01346| 
01347|    #. :dp:`fls_z6qt9obbhhcg`
01348|       Try to locate a :t:`candidate named field` of the
01349|       :t:`candidate container type`.
01350| 
01351|    #. :dp:`fls_ljnjxex3u5o`
01352|       If such a :t:`candidate named field` exists and is visible at the point
01353|       of the :t:`field access expression`, then the :t:`field access expression`
01354|       resolves to that :t:`candidate named field` and :t:`field resolution`
01355|       stops.
01356| 
01357| :dp:`fls_nm06mru40tyg`
01358| A :t:`field access expression` shall resolve to exactly one :t:`field`.
01359| 
01360| .. _fls_wqazkzle0ix9:
01361| 
01362| Method Resolution
01363| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01364| 
01365| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01366| 
01367| :dp:`fls_e5a5z5yht26l`
01368| :dt:`Method resolution` is a kind of :t:`resolution` that applies to a
01369| :t:`method call expression`.
01370| 
01371| :dp:`fls_mbdS0xiNlj92`
01372| A :dt:`receiver type` is the :t:`type` of the :dt:`receiver operand`
01373| of a :t:`method call expression`.
01374| 
01375| :dp:`fls_z80ylmlu1f3q`
01376| A :dt:`candidate receiver type` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`receiver operand`
01377| of a :t:`method call expression` :t:`under resolution`.
01378| 
01379| :dp:`fls_e1029pvq706h`
01380| A :dt:`candidate receiver type chain` is a sequence of
01381| :t:`[candidate receiver type]s`. The :t:`candidate receiver type chain` starts
01382| with the :t:`type` of the :t:`receiver operand` of the
01383| :t:`method call expression` :t:`under resolution`. From then on, the
01384| :t:`candidate receiver type chain` is treated as a :t:`dereference type chain`.
01385| 
01386| :dp:`fls_w3ik83d43fr1`
01387| A :dt:`candidate method` is a method of a :t:`candidate receiver type` that
01388| is visible from the location of the :t:`method call expression`
01389| :t:`under resolution`.
01390| 
01391| :dp:`fls_pybv4krsvktv`
01392| :t:`Method resolution` proceeds as follows:
01393| 
01394| #. :dp:`fls_m2njj6no0p1i`
01395|    For each :t:`candidate receiver type` of the
01396|    :t:`candidate receiver type chain`
01397| 
01398|    #. :dp:`fls_16l2q1wpcnbp`
01399|       Perform :t:`method resolution receiver candidate lookup` for the
01400|       :t:`candidate receiver type`.
01401| 
01402|    #. :dp:`fls_fcnahkqxomuo`
01403|       If the last :t:`candidate receiver type` is an :t:`array type`, then
01404|       perform :t:`method resolution receiver candidate lookup` for a
01405|       :t:`slice type` where the :t:`slice type` has the same :t:`element type`
01406|       as the :t:`array type`.
01407| 
01408| :dp:`fls_ii0fdpekn1qt`
01409| :dt:`Method resolution receiver candidate lookup` for a :t:`receiver type`
01410| proceeds as follows:
01411| 
01412| #. :dp:`fls_ohjmxhbw3nx3`
01413|    Perform :t:`method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for the
01414|    :t:`receiver type`.
01415| 
01416| #. :dp:`fls_lgpdicxxwq13`
01417|    Perform :t:`method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for the
01418|    :t:`immutable borrow` of the :t:`receiver type`.
01419| 
01420| #. :dp:`fls_ugl3x4y3lli2`
01421|    Perform :t:`method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for the
01422|    :t:`mutable borrow` of the :t:`receiver type`.
01423| 
01424| :dp:`fls_bb4cbmvui8fk`
01425| :dt:`Method resolution implementation candidate lookup` for a
01426| :t:`receiver type` proceeds as follows:
01427| 
01428| #. :dp:`fls_5wny1yxbyuz0`
01429|    Perform :t:`method resolution inherent implementation candidate lookup` for
01430|    the :t:`receiver type`.
01431| 
01432| #. :dp:`fls_gsc8pt4tlsqv`
01433|    Perform :t:`method resolution trait implementation candidate lookup` for the
01434|    :t:`receiver type`.
01435| 
01436| :dp:`fls_tfglce1wuq5q`
01437| :dt:`Method resolution inherent implementation candidate lookup` for a
01438| :t:`receiver type` proceeds as follows:
01439| 
01440| #. :dp:`fls_64bfcn9okeve`
01441|    Construct the :t:`dereference type chain` for the :t:`receiver type`.
01442| 
01443| #. :dp:`fls_om90v9re8b2l`
01444|    For each :t:`dereference type` in the :t:`dereference type chain`
01445| 
01446|    #. :dp:`fls_bsf4hy9x7c2e`
01447|       For each :dt:`inherent implementation` in the set of
01448|       :t:`[inherent implementation]s` of the :t:`dereference type` where the
01449|       :t:`implementing type` :t:`unifies <unify>` with the
01450|       :t:`dereference type`
01451| 
01452|       #. :dp:`fls_cnn5hkf1z5q4`
01453|          Try to locate a :t:`candidate method` in the :t:`inherent
01454|          implementation`, where the :t:`type` of the :t:`self parameter`
01455|          :t:`unifies <unify>` with the :t:`receiver type`.
01456| 
01457|       #. :dp:`fls_j9ho6xc2fj0w`
01458|          If such a :t:`candidate method` exists, then the
01459|          :t:`method call expression` resolves to that :t:`candidate method` and
01460|          :t:`method resolution` stops.
01461| 
01462| :dp:`fls_1y94elgpg0uk`
01463| :dt:`Method resolution trait implementation candidate lookup` for a
01464| :t:`receiver type` proceeds as follows:
01465| 
01466| #. :dp:`fls_npsdxrtcslcf`
01467|    Construct the :t:`dereference type chain` for the :t:`receiver type`.
01468| 
01469| #. :dp:`fls_yv5l823lwdsv`
01470|    For each :t:`dereference type` in the :t:`dereference type chain`
01471| 
01472|    #. :dp:`fls_ckdoyvbaybe0`
01473|       For each :t:`trait implementation` of the :t:`dereference type` where the
01474|       :t:`implemented trait` is :t:`in scope`
01475| 
01476|       #. :dp:`fls_1azkiu20r0e4`
01477|          Try to locate a :t:`candidate method` in the
01478|          :t:`trait implementation`, where the :t:`type` of the
01479|          :t:`self parameter` :t:`unifies <unify>` with the :t:`receiver type`.
01480|

00882| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00883| 
00884| .. _fls_fymvsy6ig99a:

00886| Attribute ``cfg``
00887| ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
00888| 
00889| .. rubric:: Syntax
00890| 
00891| .. syntax::
00892| 
00893|    CfgContent ::=
00894|        $$cfg$$ $$($$ ConfigurationPredicate $$)$$
00895| 
00896|    ConfigurationPredicate ::=
00897|        ConfigurationOption
00898|      | ConfigurationPredicateAll
00899|      | ConfigurationPredicateAny
00900|      | ConfigurationPredicateNot
00901|      | $$true$$
00902|      | $$false$$
00903| 
00904|    ConfigurationOption ::=
00905|        ConfigurationOptionName ConfigurationOptionValue?
00906| 
00907|    ConfigurationOptionName ::=
00908|        Identifier
00909| 
00910|    ConfigurationOptionValue ::=
00911|        $$=$$ StringLiteral
00912| 
00913|    ConfigurationPredicateAll ::=
00914|        $$all$$ $$($$ ConfigurationPredicateList? $$)$$
00915| 
00916|    ConfigurationPredicateAny ::=
00917|        $$any$$ $$($$ ConfigurationPredicateList? $$)$$
00918| 
00919|    ConfigurationPredicateNot ::=
00920|        $$not$$ $$($$ ConfigurationPredicate $$)$$
00921| 
00922|    ConfigurationPredicateList ::=

00937| 
00938| :dp:`fls_tncxxsyutppf`
00939| An :t:`all configuration predicate` evaluates statically to ``true`` when either
00940| all nested configuration predicates evaluate to ``true``, or there are no nested
00941| configuration predicates.
00942| 
00943| :dp:`fls_Rp73YEE3aFdI`
00944| An :dt:`any configuration predicate` is a :t:`configuration predicate` that
00945| models existential quantifier ANY.
00946| 
00947| :dp:`fls_m0zxktz168e0`
00948| An :t:`any configuration predicate` evaluates statically to ``true`` when any
00949| nested configuration predicate evaluates to ``true``.
00950| 
00951| :dp:`fls_XsxeOd32JI8x`
00952| A :dt:`not configuration predicate` is a :t:`configuration predicate` that
00953| negates the Boolean :t:`value` of its nested :t:`configuration predicate`.
00954| 
00955| :dp:`fls_tvsadfy9uibu`
00956| A :t:`not configuration predicate` evaluates statically to ``true`` when its
00957| nested configuration predicate evaluates to ``false``.
00958| 
00959| :dp:`fls_JWx8vQwl19Fu`
00960| :t:`Configuration predicate` :dc:`true` always evaluates statically to ``true``.
00961| 
00962| :dp:`fls_SziFAQsio0ab`
00963| :t:`Configuration predicate` :dc:`false` always evaluates statically to ``false``.
00964| 
00965| :dp:`fls_jbl9xyynjo0g`
00966| The :t:`evaluation` of a configuration option is tool-defined.
00967| 
00968| .. rubric:: Examples
00969| 
00970| .. code-block:: rust
00971| 
00972|    #[cfg(all(unix, target_pointer_width = "32"))]
00973|    fn on_32bit_unix() {}
00974|

01007| Derivation Attributes
01008| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01009| 
01010| .. _fls_bqw87nz4qbrb:
01011| 
01012| Attribute ``automatically_derived``
01013| ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
01014| 
01015| .. rubric:: Syntax
01016| 
01017| .. syntax::
01018| 
01019|    AutomaticallyDerivedContent ::=
01020|        $$automatically_derived$$
01021| 
01022| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01023| 
01024| :dp:`fls_5u1e0lkt0ab1`
01025| :t:`Attribute` :dc:`automatically_derived` is automatically added to
01026| :t:`[implementation]s` that are created by :t:`attribute` :c:`derive` for
01027| :t:`[built-in trait]s`.
01028| 
01029| .. _fls_r6gj1p4gajnq:
01030| 
01031| Attribute ``derive``
01032| ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
01033| 
01034| .. rubric:: Syntax
01035| 
01036| .. syntax::
01037| 
01038|    DeriveContent ::=
01039|        $$derive$$ $$($$ SimplePathList? $$)$$
01040| 
01041| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01042| 
01043| :dp:`fls_4btm6zwf445a`
01044| :t:`Attribute` :c:`derive` shall apply to :t:`[abstract data type]s`.
01045| 
01046| :dp:`fls_pjmbmj2b35y8`
01047| :t:`Attribute` :dc:`derive` lists :t:`[derive macro]s` for automatic
01048| implementation by a tool.
01049| 
01050| .. rubric:: Examples
01051| 
01052| .. code-block:: rust
01053| 
01054|    #[derive(PartialEq)]
01055|    struct S<T> {
01056|        field: T
01057|    }
01058| 
01059| :dp:`fls_xtty2ino4vwc`
01060| Attribute ``derive`` causes trait :std:`core::cmp::PartialEq` to be
01061| automatically implemented for struct ``S<T>`` as follows:
01062| 
01063| .. code-block:: rust
01064| 
01065|    impl<T: core::cmp::PartialEq> core::cmp::PartialEq for S<T> {
01066|        fn eq(&self, other: &S<T>) -> bool {
01067|           self.field == other.field
01068|        }
01069| 
01070|        fn ne(&self, other: &S<T>) -> bool {
01071|           self.field != other.field
01072|        }
01073|    }
01074|

01217| 
01218| Attribute ``link``
01219| ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
01220| 
01221| .. rubric:: Syntax
01222| 
01223| .. syntax::
01224| 
01225|    LinkContent ::=
01226|        $$link$$ $$($$ LinkOption $$)$$
01227| 
01228|    LinkOption ::=
01229|        NativeLibraryName
01230|      | NativeLibraryNameWithKind
01231|      | WebAssemblyModuleName
01232| 
01233|    NativeLibraryName ::=
01234|        $$name$$ $$=$$ StringLiteral
01235| 
01236|    NativeLibraryNameWithKind ::=
01237|        NativeLibraryName $$,$$ NativeLibraryKind
01238| 
01239|    WebAssemblyModuleName ::=
01240|        $$wasm_import_module$$ $$=$$ StringLiteral
01241| 
01242|    NativeLibraryKind ::=
01243|        $$kind$$ $$=$$ $$"$$ NativeLibraryKindType $$"$$
01244| 
01245|    NativeLibraryKindType ::=
01246|        $$dylib$$
01247|      | $$raw-dylib$$
01248|      | $$framework$$
01249|      | $$static$$
01250| 
01251| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01252| 
01253| :dp:`fls_yslpkdngo8hj`
01254| :t:`Attribute` :c:`link` shall apply to :t:`[external block]s`.
01255| 
01256| :dp:`fls_6rohnk4swj6c`
01257| :t:`Attribute` :dc:`link` shall specify the name of a native library that a tool
01258| should link with.
01259| 
01260| :dp:`fls_o83pf3bcrzma`
01261| The following native library kinds are available:
01262| 
01263| .. list-table::
01264| 
01265|    * - :dp:`fls_5541q1qoxdpf`
01266|      - **Native Library Kind**
01267|      - **Description**
01268|    * - :dp:`fls_wpqawdpevkj6`
01269|      - dylib
01270|      - Dynamic library
01271|    * - :dp:`fls_HPl6poXGiAqV`
01272|      - raw-dylib
01273|      - Dynamic library
01274|    * - :dp:`fls_h9dfs6kzmobp`
01275|      - framework
01276|      - macOS framework
01277|    * - :dp:`fls_3a3r4jf7hzqr`
01278|      - static
01279|      - Static library
01280| 
01281| :dp:`fls_1tdheukgm6ai`
01282| When :t:`attribute` :c:`link` appears without a native library kind, its native
01283| library kind defaults to ``dylib``. Native library kind framework is only valid
01284| on macOS targets.
01285| 
01286| :dp:`fls_3i9ijypnh8nx`
01287| If :t:`attribute` :c:`link` appears without a WebAssembly module name, then the
01288| WebAssembly module name defaults to ``env``.
01289| 
01290| :dp:`fls_iDjcQczFQknm`
01291| When :t:`attribute` :c:`link` appears with ``raw-dylib`` as the native library
01292| kind, the supplied name must include the file extension.
01293| 
01294| :dp:`fls_rcERq1PfKPJI`
01295| When :t:`attribute` :c:`link` appears with ``raw-dylib`` as the native library
01296| kind, the :t:`[external function]s` and :t:`[external static]s` of the related
01297| :t:`external block` shall not be subject to :t:`attribute` :c:`no_mangle`.
01298| 
01299| .. rubric:: Examples
01300| 
01301| .. code-block:: rust
01302| 
01303|    #[link(name = "CoreFoundation", kind = "framework")]
01304|    extern {}
01305|

01507| Attribute ``repr``
01508| ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
01509| 
01510| .. rubric:: Syntax
01511| 
01512| .. syntax::
01513| 
01514|    ReprContent ::=
01515|        $$repr$$ $$($$ RepresentationList $$)$$
01516| 
01517|    RepresentationList ::=
01518|        Representation ($$,$$ Representation)* $$,$$?
01519| 
01520|    Representation ::=
01521|        RepresentationKind
01522|      | Alignment
01523| 
01524|    RepresentationKind ::=
01525|        PrimitiveRepresentation
01526|      | $$C$$
01527|      | $$Rust$$
01528|      | $$transparent$$
01529| 
01530|    Alignment ::=
01531|        AlignmentDecrease
01532|      | AlignmentIncrease
01533| 
01534|    PrimitiveRepresentation ::=
01535|        $$i8$$
01536|      | $$i16$$
01537|      | $$i32$$
01538|      | $$i64$$
01539|      | $$i128$$
01540|      | $$isize$$
01541|      | $$u8$$
01542|      | $$u16$$
01543|      | $$u32$$
01544|      | $$u64$$
01545|      | $$u128$$
01546|      | $$usize$$
01547| 
01548|    AlignmentDecrease ::=
01549|        $$packed$$ AlignmentValue?
01550| 
01551|    AlignmentIncrease ::=
01552|        $$align$$ AlignmentValue
01553| 
01554|    AlignmentValue ::=
01555|        $$($$ DecimalLiteral $$)$$
01556| 
01557| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01558| 
01559| :dp:`fls_vetjq9sw84qc`
01560| :t:`Attribute` :c:`repr` shall apply to :t:`[abstract data type]s`.
01561| 
01562| :dp:`fls_is2esjz1sy36`
01563| :t:`Attribute` :dc:`repr` shall indicate the :t:`type representation` of the
01564| related :t:`type`.
01565| 
01566| :dp:`fls_8l5b6qxw0c9d`
01567| A :t:`representation modifier` is a :t:`construct` that modifies the
01568| :t:`alignment` of a :t:`type`.
01569| 
01570| .. rubric:: Examples
01571| 
01572| .. code-block:: rust
01573|

01576|        first_field: i16,

01578|    }

01580| .. _fls_19LnTi3WabFd:
01581| 
01582| Attribute ``unsafe``
01583| ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
01584| 
01585| .. rubric:: Syntax
01586| 
01587| .. syntax::
01588| 
01589|    UnsafeContent ::=
01590|        $$unsafe$$ $$($$ BuiltinAttributeContent $$)$$
01591| 
01592| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01593| 
01594| :dp:`fls_ovn9czwnwxue`
01595| An :dt:`unsafe operation` is an operation that may result in
01596| :t:`undefined behavior` that is not diagnosed as a static error.
01597| 
01598| :dp:`fls_z5DTDKM9mU3o`
01599| :t:`Attribute` :dc:`unsafe` is used to indicate that an  :t:`attribute` is :t:`unsafe <unsafe operation>`.
01600| 
01601| :dp:`fls_5pjo3nGOxbVw`
01602| :t:`Attribute` :c:`unsafe` shall apply to what the contained :t:`attribute` applies to.
01603| 
01604| :dp:`fls_gpxlWbQUNsj8`
01605| :t:`Attribute` :c:`unsafe` does not affect the behavior of the contained :t:`attribute`.
01606| 
01607| :dp:`fls_PhrfxSBvXTPV`
01608| The contained :t:`attribute` shall be one of the following:
01609| 
01610| * :dp:`fls_pCSew95bKJJ5`
01611|   :t:`Attribute` :c:`no_mangle`
01612| 
01613| * :dp:`fls_NRLgmOXxuljQ`
01614|   :t:`Attribute` :c:`export_name`

Unsafety
========

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dt:`Undefined behavior` is a situation that results in an unbounded error.

00008| Ownership and Destruction
00009| =========================
00010| 
00011| .. _fls_svkx6szhr472:
00012| 
00013| Ownership
00014| ---------
00015| 
00016| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00017| 
00018| :dp:`fls_wt81sbsecmu0`
00019| :dt:`Ownership` is a property of :t:`[value]s` that is central to the resource
00020| management model of Rust.
00021| 
00022| :dp:`fls_ckcnkbb6y3cq`
00023| An :dt:`owner` is a :t:`variable` that holds a :t:`value`.
00024| 
00025| :dp:`fls_ze0u9gfylmhn`
00026| A :t:`value` shall have only one :t:`owner`.
00027| 
00028| .. _fls_3xvm61x0t251:
00029| 
00030| Initialization
00031| --------------
00032| 
00033| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00034| 
00035| :dp:`fls_drfzu02bo7oe`
00036| :dt:`Initialization` is the act of supplying an initial :t:`value` to a
00037| :t:`variable`.
00038| 
00039| :dp:`fls_hl5tnd9yy252`
00040| A :dt:`variable` is a placeholder for a :t:`value` that is allocated on the
00041| stack.
00042| 
00043| :dp:`fls_wnhci8phdu4m`
00044| When a :t:`variable` holds a :t:`value`, the :t:`variable` is considered to be
00045| :dt:`initialized`.
00046| 
00047| :dp:`fls_ch2lvm50olqd`
00048| When a :t:`variable` lacks a :t:`value` or its :t:`value` has been
00049| :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move`, the :t:`variable` is considered
00050| to be :dt:`uninitialized`.
00051| 
00052| :dp:`fls_46910buiwvv9`
00053| A :t:`variable` shall be :t:`initialized` before it is accessed.
00054| 
00055| .. rubric:: Runtime Semantics
00056| 
00057| :dp:`fls_caufcwkpz689`
00058| All memory starts as uninitialized.
00059|

00061| 
00062| :dp:`fls_4lg92b9yima6`

00064| 
00065| .. code-block:: rust
00066| 
00067|    let a: i32 = 42;

00069| :dp:`fls_xn1au0blioa3`
00070| Variable ``b`` starts off as uninitialized, but is later initialized by virtue

00078| :dp:`fls_jmcjboopvytb`

00085|

00087|    let d: AtomicI32 = c;

00089| .. _fls_v5x85lt5ulva:
00090| 
00091| References
00092| ----------
00093| 
00094| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00095| 
00096| :dp:`fls_7x9pi2o7pee7`
00097| A :t:`reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`reference type`. A :t:`reference`
00098| can be obtained explicitly by using a :t:`borrow expression` or implicitly in
00099| certain scenarios.
00100| 
00101| :dp:`fls_tsqvr3fmcel`
00102| A :dt:`referent` is the :t:`value` pointed-to by a :t:`reference`.
00103| 
00104| :dp:`fls_ev4a82fdhwr8`
00105| A :t:`reference` shall point to an :t:`initialized` :t:`referent`.
00106| 
00107| :dp:`fls_cckf6dtkgwb4`
00108| The :t:`lifetime` of a :t:`referent` shall be at least as long as the
00109| :t:`lifetime` of its :t:`reference`.
00110| 
00111| :dp:`fls_8kqb8754e6p4`
00112| A :t:`reference` is :dt:`active` from the point of obtaining its :t:`referent`
00113| upto the last use of the :t:`reference`, prior to another assignment to the
00114| :t:`reference` or the end of the :t:`scope` of the :t:`reference`.
00115| 
00116| :dp:`fls_v69rptdjao42`
00117| A :t:`referent` shall not be :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move` while
00118| a :t:`reference` to it is :t:`active`.
00119| 
00120| :dp:`fls_vg9h6tz6z37w`
00121| A :t:`referent` shall not be modified while a :t:`reference` to it is
00122| :t:`active`.
00123| 
00124| :dp:`fls_wcf5mxrzbujn`
00125| An :t:`immutable reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`shared reference type`, and
00126| prevents the mutation of its :t:`referent`.
00127| 
00128| :dp:`fls_4m2v8k1q9p0s`
00129| A :t:`shared reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`shared reference type`.
00130| 
00131| :dp:`fls_fckoj1jh5mrc`
00132| A :t:`mutable reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`mutable reference type`, and
00133| allows the mutation of its :t:`referent`.
00134| 
00135| :dp:`fls_hqxsuyn285he`
00136| The :t:`referent` of an :t:`immutable reference` shall be mutated only when the
00137| :t:`type` of the :t:`referent` is subject to :t:`interior mutability`.
00138| 
00139| :dp:`fls_i1ny0k726a4a`
00140| While a :t:`mutable reference` is :t:`active`, no other :t:`reference` shall
00141| refer to a :t:`value` that :t:`[overlap]s` with the :t:`referent` of the
00142| :t:`mutable reference`.

00145| 
00146| :dp:`fls_eT1hnLOx6vxk`

00151| 
00152| .. code-block:: rust
00153| 
00154|    let immutable_reference: &i32 = &42;
00155|    let mutable_reference: &mut i32 = &mut 42;
00156| 
00157| .. _fls_a14slch83hzn:
00158| 
00159| Borrowing
00160| ---------
00161| 
00162| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00163| 
00164| :dp:`fls_c02flohk54pc`
00165| :dt:`Borrowing` is the process of temporarily associating a :t:`reference` with a
00166| :t:`value` without transferring :t:`ownership` permanently.
00167| 
00168| :dp:`fls_j9kof0px3l7s`
00169| A :t:`borrow` is a :t:`reference` produced by :t:`borrowing`.
00170| 
00171| :dp:`fls_yL6cMahJUIqO`
00172| A :t:`value` is :t:`borrowed` when it is associated with an active
00173| :t:`borrow`.
00174| 
00175| :dp:`fls_zepwytjwy049`
00176| An :t:`implicit borrow` is a :t:`borrow` that is not present syntactically in
00177| program text. An :t:`implicit borrow` occurs in the following contexts:
00178| 
00179| * :dp:`fls_nordokzfy36d`
00180|   The :t:`call operand` of a :t:`call expression`,
00181| 
00182| * :dp:`fls_bjf3futso849`
00183|   The :t:`[operand]s` of a :t:`comparison expression`,
00184| 
00185| * :dp:`fls_yfmy4v5zlgw9`
00186|   The :t:`assigned operand` of a :t:`compound assignment expression`,
00187| 
00188| * :dp:`fls_jv18y618j2s3`
00189|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`field access expression`,
00190| 
00191| * :dp:`fls_g4i0jb27iryr`
00192|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression`,
00193| 
00194| * :dp:`fls_kky9ufexrvaw`
00195|   The :t:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression`,
00196| 
00197| * :dp:`fls_o5oq4jfswr4q`
00198|   The :t:`receiver operand` of a :t:`method call expression`.
00199| 
00200| :dp:`fls_MT69AGRXH9pS``
00201| An :t:`implicit borrow` may be an :dt:`immutable borrow` or a :t:`mutable borrow`
00202| if required.
00203| 
00204| :dp:`fls_hyl4bdjbuzbw`
00205| An :t:`immutable borrow` is an :t:`immutable reference` produced by
00206| :t:`borrowing`.
00207| 
00208| :dp:`fls_8q0n7v3k2m1b`
00209| A :dt:`shared borrow` is a :t:`borrow` produced by evaluating an
00210| :t:`immutable borrow expression`.
00211| 
00212| :dp:`fls_pu19i4sj6yg0`
00213| A :dt:`mutable borrow` is a :t:`mutable reference` produced by :t:`borrowing`.
00214| 
00215| :dp:`fls_kxws4zmaahj6`
00216| :t:`Borrowing` a :t:`field` of a :t:`union type` borrows all remaining
00217| :t:`[field]s` using the same :t:`lifetime`.
00218| 
00219| :dp:`fls_kup2ou22nwyl`
00220| Immutably :t:`borrowing` a :t:`value` proceeds as follows:
00221| 
00222| #. :dp:`fls_8q5ly4x104ai`
00223|    An :t:`immutable borrow` of :t:`type` ``&'a T`` is created, where
00224|    :t:`lifetime` ``'a`` is replaced by a :t:`lifetime variable`, and
00225|    ``T`` is replaced by the borrowed :t:`type`.
00226| 
00227| #. :dp:`fls_yhchu2bpil4m`
00228|    Lifetime inference is performed.
00229| 
00230| #. :dp:`fls_568o7nyihndd`
00231|    The :t:`immutable borrow` is checked against other :t:`[borrow]s` and
00232|    :t:`by move` passing within the enclosing :t:`item`.
00233| 
00234| #. :dp:`fls_Z2n7EJiLY5CT`
00235|    An :t:`immutable reference` to the borrowed :t:`value` is produced.
00236| 
00237| #. :dp:`fls_ibi9mqGeHNLp`
00238|    The :t:`immutable borrow` is released immediately after the last usage of its
00239|    related :t:`immutable reference`.
00240| 
00241| :dp:`fls_f9we73i8vwq3`
00242| Mutably :t:`borrowing` a :t:`value` proceeds as follows:
00243| 
00244| #. :dp:`fls_w5bjgaov8w60`
00245|    A :t:`mutable borrow` of :t:`type` ``&'a mut T`` is created, where
00246|    :t:`lifetime` ``'a`` is replaced by a :t:`lifetime variable`, and
00247|    ``T`` is replaced by the borrowed :t:`type`.
00248| 
00249| #. :dp:`fls_gbqizu6gu6kk`
00250|    Lifetime inference is performed.
00251| 
00252| #. :dp:`fls_ovkkxeybumvt`
00253|    The :t:`mutable borrow` is checked against other :t:`[borrow]s` and
00254|    :t:`by move` passing within the enclosing :t:`item`.
00255| 
00256| #. :dp:`fls_7Kzu0qXiBUCb`
00257|    A :t:`mutable reference` to the borrowed :t:`value` is produced.
00258| 
00259| #. :dp:`fls_VDHBP4oAoqT5`
00260|    The :t:`mutable borrow` is released immediately after the last usage of its
00261|    related :t:`mutable reference`.
00262| 
00263| .. rubric:: Examples
00264| 
00265| .. code-block:: rust
00266| 
00267|    let mutable_borrow = &mut 42;
00268|    let immutable_borrow = &42;
00269| 
00270| .. _fls_77scxuomlbgs:
00271| 
00272| Passing Conventions
00273| -------------------
00274| 
00275| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00276| 
00277| :dp:`fls_fvwx2ufeyzcs`
00278| A :dt:`passing convention` is the mechanism that defines how a :t:`value` is
00279| transferred between :t:`[place]s`.
00280| 
00281| :dp:`fls_h2pgsij1rbms`
00282| A :t:`copy type` is a :t:`type` that implements the :std:`core::marker::Copy`
00283| :t:`trait`.
00284| 
00285| :dp:`fls_yx2knbby70fy`
00286| A :t:`value` of a :t:`copy type` is :t:`passed <passing convention>`
00287| :dt:`by copy`. Passing :t:`by copy` does not change the :t:`owner` of the
00288| :t:`value`.
00289| 
00290| :dp:`fls_6ul3f6v0foma`
00291| A :dt:`move type` is a :t:`type` that implements the :std:`core::marker::Sized`
00292| :t:`trait` and is not a :t:`copy type`.
00293| 
00294| :dp:`fls_3ztdz02efeoc`
00295| A :t:`value` of a :t:`move type` is :t:`passed <passing convention>`
00296| :dt:`by move`. Passing :t:`by move` changes the :t:`owner` of the :t:`value`.
00297| 
00298| :dp:`fls_konzgoybhfqm`
00299| A :t:`value` of a :t:`place expression` shall be
00300| :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move` only when it denotes:
00301| 
00302| * :dp:`fls_4bnbv7mqod57`
00303|   A :t:`field` of a :t:`place expression` that can be
00304|   :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move` and whose :t:`type` does not
00305|   implement the :std:`core::ops::Drop` :t:`trait`, or
00306| 
00307| * :dp:`fls_3xk3p1unbjy5`
00308|   A :t:`temporary`, or
00309| 
00310| * :dp:`fls_vk1xhvdaakh0`
00311|   A :t:`variable` which is not currently :t:`borrowed`.
00312| 
00313| :dp:`fls_vveEJn7lngT8`
00314| A :t:`value` of a :t:`value expression` is always
00315| :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move`.
00316| 
00317| :dp:`fls_gq35gqagw35`
00318| A :t:`value` not subject to :t:`by copy` or :t:`by move` :t:`passing convention`
00319| shall not be :t:`passed <passing convention>` between :t:`[place]s`.
00320| 
00321| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00322| 
00323| :dp:`fls_cfqzpmnfeh8h`
00324| Passing a :t:`value` :t:`by copy` from a source :t:`owner` to a target
00325| :t:`owner` proceeds as follows:
00326| 
00327| #. :dp:`fls_go9gdlk5d3km`
00328|    The :t:`value` of the source :t:`owner` is copied.
00329| 
00330| #. :dp:`fls_459xx6febmf0`
00331|    The copy is assigned to the target :t:`owner`.
00332| 
00333| :dp:`fls_3xyq50abdiv6`
00334| Passing a :t:`value` :t:`by move` from a source :t:`owner` to a target
00335| :t:`owner` proceeds as follows:
00336| 
00337| #. :dp:`fls_7kcx3u8gvl1d`
00338|    The :t:`value` is unassigned from the source :t:`owner`.
00339| 
00340| #. :dp:`fls_i4hrifsb9msr`
00341|    The :t:`value` is assigned to the target :t:`owner`.
00342| 
00343| .. rubric:: Examples
00344| 
00345| :dp:`fls_7tadh1zel0fc`
00346| Type ``i32`` is a copy type. By the end of the second let statement, ``x`` is
00347| the owner of the original ``42`` and ``y`` is the owner of a cloned ``42``.
00348| 
00349| .. code-block:: rust
00350| 
00351|    let x: i32 = 42;
00352|    let y: i32 = x;
00353| 
00354| :dp:`fls_ywt328hcieka`
00355| Type :std:`core::sync::atomic::AtomicI32` is a move type. By the end of the
00356| second let statement, ``x`` is uninitialized and ``y`` is the sole owner of the
00357| atomic ``42``.
00358| 
00359| .. code-block:: rust
00360| 
00361|    use core::sync::atomic::AtomicI32;
00362| 
00363|    let x: AtomicI32 = AtomicI32::new(42);
00364|    let y: AtomicI32 = x;
00365| 
00366| .. _fls_4jiw35pan7vn:
00367| 
00368| Destruction
00369| -----------
00370| 
00371| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00372| 
00373| :dp:`fls_e7ucq87s806d`
00374| :dt:`Destruction` is the process of recovering resources associated with a
00375| :t:`value` as it goes out of scope.
00376| 
00377| .. _fls_u2mzjgiwbkz0:
00378| 
00379| Destructors
00380| -----------
00381| 
00382| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00383| 
00384| :dp:`fls_9m0gszdle0qb`
00385| A :dt:`drop type` is a :t:`type` that implements the :std:`core::ops::Drop`
00386| :t:`trait` or contains a :t:`field` that has a :t:`drop type`.
00387| 
00388| :dp:`fls_4nkzidytpi6`
00389| A :t:`destructor` is a :t:`function` that is invoked immediately before the
00390| :t:`destruction` of a :t:`value` of a :t:`drop type`.
00391| 
00392| :dp:`fls_wzuwapjqtyyy`
00393| :dt:`Dropping` a :t:`value` is the act of invoking the :t:`destructor` of the
00394| related :t:`type`.
00395| 
00396| :dp:`fls_8U45VMMfCU7F`
00397| A :t:`value` is :dt:`dropped` when its :t:`destructor` is invoked.
00398| 
00399| :dp:`fls_gfvm70iqu1l4`
00400| An :t:`uninitialized` :t:`variable` is not :t:`dropped`.
00401| 
00402| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00403| 
00404| :dp:`fls_l2xkdjeydqtx`
00405| :t:`Dropping` an :t:`initialized` :t:`variable` proceeds as follows:
00406| 
00407| #. :dp:`fls_bync24y6gp93`
00408|    If the :t:`drop type` implements the :std:`core::ops::Drop` :t:`trait`, then
00409|    ``core::ops::Drop::drop`` of the :t:`drop type` is invoked.
00410| 
00411| #. :dp:`fls_jzancf72i95f`
00412|    If the :t:`drop type` is an :t:`array type`, then its elements are
00413|    :t:`dropped` from the first element to the last element.
00414| 
00415| #. :dp:`fls_gjn2jnsal9gs`
00416|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`closure type`, then all
00417|    :t:`[capture target]s` whose :t:`capture mode` is :t:`by move` are
00418|    :t:`dropped` in unspecified order.
00419| 
00420| #. :dp:`fls_ol2w2292frfi`
00421|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is an :t:`enum type`, then the :t:`[field]s`
00422|    of the active :t:`enum variant` are :t:`dropped` in declaration order.
00423| 
00424| #. :dp:`fls_6ii5o68vuymj`
00425|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`slice type`, then its elements are
00426|    :t:`dropped` from the first element to the last element.
00427| 
00428| #. :dp:`fls_sup43es8ps8r`
00429|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`struct type`, then its
00430|    :t:`[field]s` are :t:`dropped` in declaration order.
00431| 
00432| #. :dp:`fls_y9q0eqr865b3`
00433|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`trait object type`, then the
00434|    :t:`destructor` of the underlying :t:`type` is invoked.
00435| 
00436| #. :dp:`fls_kdqng6eovxns`
00437|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`tuple type`, then its :t:`[field]s`
00438|    are :t:`dropped` in declaration order.
00439| 
00440| #. :dp:`fls_ag249y74jg6c`
00441|    Otherwise, :t:`dropping` has no effect.
00442| 
00443| .. rubric:: Examples
00444| 
00445| .. code-block:: rust
00446| 
00447|    struct PrintOnDrop(&'static str);
00448| 
00449|    impl core::ops::Drop for PrintOnDrop {
00450|        fn drop(&mut self) {
00451|            println!("{}", self.0);
00452|        }
00453|    }
00454| 
00455| :dp:`fls_tw36n3g32a0y`
00456| When object ``array`` is dropped, its destructor drops the first element, then
00457| the second element.
00458| 
00459| .. code-block:: rust
00460| 
00461|    let array = [PrintOnDrop("first element to be dropped"),
00462|                 PrintOnDrop("second element to be dropped")];
00463| 
00464| :dp:`fls_fmn33zhorkf`
00465| Object ``uninitialized`` is not dropped.
00466| 
00467| .. code-block:: rust
00468| 
00469|    let uninitialized: PrintOnDrop;
00470| 
00471| .. _fls_rm4ncoopcdvj:
00472| 
00473| Drop Scopes
00474| -----------
00475| 
00476| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00477| 
00478| :dp:`fls_7uav7vkcv4pz`
00479| A :dt:`drop scope` is a region of program text that governs the :t:`dropping` of
00480| :t:`[value]s`. When control flow leaves a :t:`drop scope`, all :t:`[value]s`
00481| associated with that :t:`drop scope` are :t:`dropped` based on a
00482| :t:`drop order`.
00483| 
00484| :dp:`fls_vWILlWIfhB69`
00485| :dt:`Drop order` is the order by which :t:`[value]s` are :t:`dropped` when a
00486| :t:`drop scope` is left.
00487| 
00488| :dp:`fls_txvxrn6wbyql`
00489| A :dt:`drop construct` is a :t:`construct` that employs a :t:`drop scope`. The
00490| following :t:`[construct]s` are :t:`[drop construct]s`:
00491| 
00492| * :dp:`fls_n6y6brm6pghr`
00493|   :t:`[Expression]s`,
00494| 
00495| * :dp:`fls_gdh6wwvi7ci6`
00496|   :t:`[Function]s`,
00497| 
00498| * :dp:`fls_owqk2fcpvc4s`
00499|   A :t:`match arm` of a :t:`match expression`,
00500| 
00501| * :dp:`fls_ckh8wkq0y5ja`
00502|   :t:`[Statement]s`.
00503| 
00504| :dp:`fls_2zwwnzepgmje`
00505| :t:`[Drop scope]s` are nested within one another as follows:
00506| 
00507| * :dp:`fls_vlbx5ukw5c8l`
00508|   The :t:`drop scope` of a :t:`function` is the outermost :t:`drop scope`.
00509| 
00510| * :dp:`fls_d5yg6w8gv6aq`
00511|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of a :t:`function body` is the :t:`drop scope` of
00512|   its related :t:`function`.
00513| 
00514| * :dp:`fls_qidma4fpkhb0`
00515|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of an :t:`operand` in an :t:`expression statement`
00516|   is the :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`expression statement`.
00517| 
00518| * :dp:`fls_1o9ye6cwoyiq`
00519|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`expression` of a :t:`let statement` is
00520|   the :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`let statement`.
00521| 
00522| * :dp:`fls_16htxf824xbk`
00523|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of a :t:`statement` is the :t:`drop scope` of the
00524|   :t:`block expression` that contains the :t:`statement`.
00525| 
00526| * :dp:`fls_lbsfhg42yiqy`
00527|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`operand` of a :t:`match arm guard` is
00528|   the :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`match arm` that contains the
00529|   :t:`match arm guard`.
00530| 
00531| * :dp:`fls_5m3u3k6f00bd`
00532|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`operand` of a :t:`match arm` is the
00533|   :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`match arm` that contains the :t:`operand`.
00534| 
00535| * :dp:`fls_m86ljncnmo7j`
00536|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of a :t:`match arm` is the :t:`drop scope` of the
00537|   related :t:`match expression`.
00538| 
00539| * :dp:`fls_bewcu5xceu8i`
00540|   The parent :t:`drop scope` of all other :t:`[drop scope]s` is the
00541|   :t:`drop scope` of the immediately enclosing :t:`expression`.
00542| 
00543| :dp:`fls_iHHhlxCiqNWZ`
00544| A :t:`binding` declared in a :t:`for loop expression` is associated with
00545| the :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`block expression` of the
00546| :t:`for loop expression`.
00547| 
00548| :dp:`fls_swXXkbZGLPKa`
00549| A :t:`binding` declared in an :t:`if let expression` is associated with the
00550| :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`block expression` of the :t:`if let expression`.
00551| 
00552| :dp:`fls_vrqgac634wpr`
00553| A :t:`binding` declared in a :t:`let statement` is associated with the
00554| :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`block expression` that contains the
00555| :t:`let statement`.
00556| 
00557| :dp:`fls_fnvr5w2wzxns`
00558| A :t:`binding` declared in a :t:`match expression` is associated with the
00559| :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`match arm` of the :t:`match expression`.
00560| 
00561| :dp:`fls_1vZFa5mDEbXW`
00562| A :t:`binding` declared in a :t:`while let loop expression` is associated with
00563| the :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`block expression` of the
00564| :t:`while let loop expression`.
00565| 
00566| :dp:`fls_3jtU8grRaMM9`
00567| A :t:`value` or :t:`binding` of a :t:`function parameter` is associated with the
00568| :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`function` of the :t:`function parameter`.
00569| 
00570| :dp:`fls_8r39duatupxw`
00571| A :t:`temporary` that is not subject to :t:`constant promotion` is associated
00572| with the innermost :t:`drop scope` that contains the :t:`expression` which
00573| produced the :t:`temporary`, taking into account :t:`drop scope extension`. The
00574| possible :t:`[drop scope]s` are as follows:
00575| 
00576| * :dp:`fls_2peq1tihglnr`
00577|   The :t:`drop scope` of a :t:`function body`.
00578| 
00579| * :dp:`fls_il09n7sq0a3k`
00580|   The :t:`drop scope` of a :t:`statement`.
00581| 
00582| * :dp:`fls_s1wbqld139gz`
00583|   The :t:`drop scope` of a :t:`block expression` of an :t:`if expression`, an
00584|   :t:`infinite loop expression`, or a :t:`while loop expression`.
00585| 
00586| * :dp:`fls_asvuef2pc3m0`
00587|   The :t:`drop scope` of an :t:`else expression`.
00588| 
00589| * :dp:`fls_560437qmeqtr`
00590|   The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`subject expression` of an :t:`if expression`.
00591| 
00592| * :dp:`fls_8cunkfc6x24q`
00593|   The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`iteration expression` of a
00594|   :t:`while loop expression`.
00595| 
00596| * :dp:`fls_n108lvc4otoc`
00597|   The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`operand` of a :t:`match arm`.
00598| 
00599| * :dp:`fls_ptk6yibqyfzi`
00600|   The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`operand` of a :t:`match arm guard`.
00601| 
00602| * :dp:`fls_dltmd8e8c5ia`
00603|   The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`right operand` of a
00604|   :t:`lazy boolean expression`.
00605| 
00606| .. _fls_5eima0pd31c0:
00607| 
00608| Drop Scope Extension
00609| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00610| 
00611| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00612| 
00613| :dp:`fls_kflqez2mtbit`
00614| :dt:`Drop scope extension` is the process of extending a :t:`drop scope`
00615| associated with a :t:`temporary` to prevent the premature :t:`dropping` of the
00616| :t:`temporary`.
00617| 
00618| :dp:`fls_xjw82bujm148`
00619| An :dt:`extending pattern` is either
00620| 
00621| * :dp:`fls_965wt48ooqyw`
00622|   A :dt:`reference identifier pattern`, or
00623| 
00624| * :dp:`fls_r8nt0zp8dnyp`
00625|   A :t:`slice pattern`, a :t:`struct pattern`, :t:`tuple pattern` or a
00626|   :t:`tuple struct pattern` that contains at least one :t:`subpattern` that is
00627|   an :t:`extending pattern`.
00628| 
00629| :dp:`fls_3ycn4u1fe9h`
00630| If the :dt:`pattern-without-alternation` of a :t:`let statement` is an
00631| :t:`extending pattern`, then the :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`expression` of
00632| the :t:`let statement` is extended to the :t:`drop scope` of the
00633| :t:`block expression` that contains the :t:`let statement`.
00634| 
00635| :dp:`fls_YeeZWqTdMivX`
00636| An :dt:`extending expression` is an :t:`expression` with an
00637| :t:`extended drop scope <drop scope extension>`.
00638| 
00639| :dp:`fls_wyzau8hhq74d`
00640| An :t:`extending expression` is any of the following:
00641| 
00642| * :dp:`fls_ju7cpftd8r2g`
00643|   The :t:`expression` of a :t:`let statement`.
00644| 
00645| * :dp:`fls_gjd1ow3l7swe`
00646|   The :t:`operand` of an :t:`extending expression` that is an
00647|   :t:`array expression`, a :t:`borrow expression`, a :t:`struct expression`, a
00648|   :t:`tuple expression`, or a :t:`type cast expression`.
00649| 
00650| * :dp:`fls_Rj9zhVutfQod`
00651|   The :t:`block expression` and :t:`else expression` of an :t:`if expression`
00652|   that is an :t:`extending expression`.
00653| 
00654| * :dp:`fls_oodpp3LpXC13`
00655|   The :t:`expression` of an :t:`else expression` that is an :t:`extending
00656|   expression`.
00657| 
00658| * :dp:`fls_xGThCPoTUSAi`
00659|   The :t:`expression` of a :t:`match arm` of a :t:`match expression` that is an
00660|   :t:`extending expression`.
00661| 
00662| * :dp:`fls_iqw0d1l1lj3i`
00663|   The :t:`tail expression` of a :t:`block expression` that is an
00664|   :t:`extending expression`.
00665| 
00666| * :dp:`fls_eeaJtK4w5gVK`
00667|   The :t:`[argument operand]s` of a :t:`tuple struct call expression`
00668|   that is an :t:`extending expression`.
00669| 
00670| :dp:`fls_aq01wjpkxhq9`
00671| The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`operand` of a :t:`borrow expression` that is an
00672| :t:`extending expression` is extended to the :t:`drop scope` of the
00673| :t:`block expression` that contains the :t:`let statement`.
00674| 
00675| :dp:`fls_VDPi1dJzJMUb`
00676| The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`operand` of a :t:`borrow expression`, a
00677| :t:`dereference expression`, or a :t:`field access expression` that has an
00678| extended :t:`drop scope` is extended to the :t:`drop scope` of the
00679| :t:`expression`.
00680| 
00681| :dp:`fls_elGH7HSawMRb`
00682| The :t:`drop scope` of the :t:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression` that
00683| has an extended :t:`drop scope` is extended to the :t:`drop scope` of the
00684| :t:`expression`.
00685| 
00686| .. rubric:: Examples
00687| 
00688| :dp:`fls_29y59x8bmw75`
00689| See :p:`fls_u2mzjgiwbkz0` for the declaration of ``PrintOnDrop``.
00690| 
00691| :dp:`fls_subo2w7ln43q`
00692| The drop scope of the temporary created for expression ``AtomicI32::new(42)`` is
00693| extended to the drop scope of the block expression.
00694| 
00695| .. code-block:: rust
00696| 
00697|    use core::sync::atomic::AtomicI32;
00698| 
00699|    {
00700|        let ref mut a = AtomicI32::new(42);
00701|        println!("{}", a);
00702|    }
00703| 
00704| .. _fls_afafmafz4hf2:
00705| 
00706| Drop Order
00707| ----------
00708| 
00709| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00710| 
00711| :dp:`fls_n6o1xzjiz8cv`
00712| :t:`Drop order` is the order by which :t:`[value]s` are :t:`dropped` when a
00713| :t:`drop scope` is left.
00714| 
00715| :dp:`fls_jwofws3022ar`
00716| When a :t:`drop scope` is left, all :t:`[value]s` associated with that
00717| :t:`drop scope` are :t:`dropped` as follows:
00718| 
00719| * :dp:`fls_W2S2FrkuedYC`
00720|   :t:`[Binding]s` introduced by an :t:`or-pattern` are dropped in reverse
00721|   declaration order, where the declaration order is defined by the first

Ownership
---------

management model of Rust.

00405| Place Expressions
00406| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00407| 
00408| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00409| 
00410| :dp:`fls_yBEzAdfSAMYQ`
00411| A :dt:`place` is a location where a :t:`value` resides.
00412| 
00413| :dp:`fls_qbrcg3cl9td`
00414| A :dt:`place expression` is an :t:`expression` that represents a memory
00415| location. The following :t:`[expression]s` are :t:`[place expression]s`:
00416| 
00417| * :dp:`fls_jpmhibm4omm7`
00418|   :t:`[Dereference expression]s`,
00419| 
00420| * :dp:`fls_none1dykbn8c`
00421|   :t:`[Field access expression]s`,
00422| 
00423| * :dp:`fls_lj7x5dgbmg9i`
00424|   :t:`[Index expression]s`,
00425| 
00426| * :dp:`fls_anzidgx02lly`
00427|   :t:`[Parenthesized expression]s` where the :t:`operand` is a
00428|   :t:`place expression`,
00429| 
00430| * :dp:`fls_ya05djl1d154`
00431|   :t:`[Path expression]s` that resolve to a :t:`static` or a :t:`variable`.
00432| 
00433| * :dp:`fls_gv4M0DE3OMwk`
00434|   A :dt:`temporary`.
00435| 
00436| :dp:`fls_ku38h562vfyl`
00437| A :dt:`mutable place expression` is a :t:`place expression` whose memory
00438| location can be modified. The following :t:`[place expression]s` are
00439| :t:`[mutable place expression]s`:
00440| 
00441| * :dp:`fls_1tq2o2huda9l`
00442|   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` implements the
00443|   :std:`core::ops::DerefMut` :t:`trait`,
00444| 
00445| * :dp:`fls_6b4rwkrc1ap6`
00446|   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` is a
00447|   :t:`mutable raw pointer type` (a :t:`raw pointer type` subject to
00448|   :t:`keyword` ``mut``),
00449| 
00450| * :dp:`fls_s4bhrpykzmm7`
00451|   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` is a
00452|   :t:`mutable reference type` (a :t:`reference type` subject to :t:`keyword`
00453|   ``mut``),
00454| 
00455| * :dp:`fls_xm0gm2q27x2e`
00456|   A :t:`field access expression` where the :t:`container operand` is a
00457|   :t:`mutable place expression`,
00458| 
00459| * :dp:`fls_bt50fltfqcvn`
00460|   An :dt:`index expression` whose :t:`type` implements the
00461|   :std:`core::ops::IndexMut` :t:`trait`,
00462| 
00463| * :dp:`fls_Sgfxp186GMvz`
00464|   :t:`[Parenthesized expression]s` where the :t:`operand` is a
00465|   :t:`mutable place expression`,
00466| 
00467| * :dp:`fls_ilaqmj3hc5uv`
00468|   A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :dt:`mutable static`,
00469| 
00470| * :dp:`fls_m0gbw9myylv2`
00471|   A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :dt:`mutable variable` that is not
00472|   currently borrowed,
00473| 
00474| * :dp:`fls_dcm3yr3y9y0a`
00475|   A :t:`temporary`.
00476| 
00477| :dp:`fls_cPEMHZtPkctX`
00478| An :dt:`immutable place expression` is a :t:`place expression` whose memory
00479| location cannot be modified. All :t:`[place expression]s` that are not
00480| :t:`[mutable place expression]s` are :t:`[immutable place expression]s`.
00481| 
00482| :dp:`fls_4vxi1ji93dxb`
00483| A :dt:`place expression context` is a :t:`construct` that may evaluate its
00484| :t:`operand` as a memory location.
00485| 
00486| :dp:`fls_fzsrdrHnndRd`
00487| The following :t:`[construct]s` are :t:`[place expression context]s`:
00488| 
00489| * :dp:`fls_ZED5wJQVO6nf`

00496| * :dp:`fls_K7SbApHPmwjM`
00497|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression`,
00498| 
00499| * :dp:`fls_KxWIzoh9WwK7`
00500|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression`,
00501| 
00502| * :dp:`fls_oV9Hd6OiFAZX`
00503|   The :t:`container operand` of a :t:`field access expression`,
00504| 
00505| * :dp:`fls_NnGiNsH6Zgmp`
00506|   The initialization :t:`expression` of a :t:`let statement`,
00507| 
00508| * :dp:`fls_jLZlxIHr4w2v`
00509|   The :t:`operand` of an :dt:`implicit borrow`,
00510| 
00511| * :dp:`fls_giZ7w1G02JSg`
00512|   The :dt:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression`,
00513| 
00514| * :dp:`fls_5yXuTLQOQ3cc`

00521| * :dp:`fls_JBfZuFDQg3mU`

00545|   The :t:`container operand` of :t:`field access expression` if evaluated in a

00549|   The :t:`subject let expression` of an :t:`if let expression` or a

00552| * :dp:`fls_o76QXHyrrJPG`
00553|   The :t:`operand` of a mutable :t:`implicit borrow`,
00554| 
00555| * :dp:`fls_ka5b87tkf8t6`
00556|   The initialization :t:`expression` of a :t:`let statement`,
00557| 
00558| * :dp:`fls_brwv1zwu37e8`
00559|   The :t:`subject expression` of a :t:`match expression`,
00560| 
00561| :dp:`fls_YHj3sB09Wbyb`
00562| An :dt:`immutable place expression context` is a :t:`place expression context`
00563| that may evaluate its :t:`operand` as an immutable memory location. All
00564| :t:`[place expression context]s` that are not
00565| :t:`[mutable place expression context]s` are

00576| Value Expressions
00577| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00578| 
00579| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00580| 
00581| :dp:`fls_7q4hrt6yfr9b`
00582| A :dt:`value expression` is an :t:`expression` that represents a :t:`value`.
00583| All :t:`[expression]s` that are not :t:`[place expression]s` are
00584| :t:`[value expression]s`.
00585| 
00586| :dp:`fls_pB6xlp4uAg37`
00587| A :dt:`value expression context` is an expression context that is not a
00588| :t:`place expression context`.
00589| 
00590| :dp:`fls_8uhfwqurbyqf`
00591| The evaluation of a :t:`value expression` in a :t:`place expression context`
00592| shall evaluate the :t:`value expression` as a :t:`temporary` and then use the
00593| :t:`temporary` in the :t:`place expression context`.
00594|

00598| -------------------

00635| Path Expressions
00636| ----------------
00637| 
00638| .. rubric:: Syntax
00639| 
00640| .. syntax::
00641| 
00642|    PathExpression ::=
00643|        UnqualifiedPathExpression
00644|      | QualifiedPathExpression
00645| 
00646| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00647| 
00648| :dp:`fls_gvanx4874ycy`
00649| A :dt:`path expression` is an :t:`expression` that denotes a :t:`path`.
00650| 
00651| :dp:`fls_EOkrcIj9CuhV`
00652| A :t:`path expression` shall resolve to either a :t:`constant parameter`, a
00653| :t:`constant`, a :t:`function`, a :t:`static`, a :t:`tuple enum variant`, a
00654| :t:`tuple struct`, a :t:`unit enum variant`, a :t:`unit struct`, or a
00655| :t:`variable`.
00656| 
00657| :dp:`fls_4q6m9t1w7zpa`
00658| A :dt:`unit struct` is a :t:`struct` without a :t:`field list`.
00659| 
00660| :dp:`fls_gz67ju6l7uhn`
00661| A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :t:`mutable static` shall require
00662| :t:`unsafe context`.
00663| 
00664| :dp:`fls_cjywisyiyti6`
00665| The :t:`type` of a :t:`path expression` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`entity` that
00666| it resolved to.
00667| 
00668| :dp:`fls_5ifai8nkp5ek`
00669| The :t:`value` of a :t:`path expression` is the :t:`entity` that it resolved to.
00670| 
00671| .. rubric:: Examples
00672| 
00673| .. code-block:: rust
00674| 
00675|    globals::STATIC_VARIABLE
00676|    Vec::<i32>::push

00678| .. _fls_hndm19t57wby:
00679| 
00680| Block Expressions
00681| -----------------
00682| 
00683| .. rubric:: Syntax
00684| 
00685| .. syntax::
00686| 
00687|    BlockExpression ::=
00688|        $${$$
00689|          InnerAttributeOrDoc*
00690|          StatementList
00691|        $$}$$
00692| 
00693|    StatementList ::=
00694|        Statement* Expression?
00695| 
00696| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00697| 
00698| :dp:`fls_nf65p0l0v0gr`
00699| A :dt:`block expression` is an :t:`expression` that sequences :t:`[expression]s`
00700| and :t:`[statement]s`.
00701| 
00702| :dp:`fls_tn3hj7k2lliu`
00703| A :dt:`tail expression` is the last :t:`expression` within a :t:`block
00704| expression`.
00705| 
00706| :dp:`fls_DfCne8YWevLE`
00707| When the remaining :t:`[lexical element]s` of a :s:`StatementList` match either
00708| an :s:`Expression` or :s:`Statement` they are interpreted as an :s:`Expression`.
00709| 
00710| :dp:`fls_u4gj2lnkq9ub`
00711| The :t:`type` of a :t:`block expression` is determined as follows:
00712| 
00713| * :dp:`fls_ltEygvWDtHXE`
00714|   If the :t:`block expression` contains at least one :t:`break expression` and
00715|   has a :t:`tail expression`, then the :t:`type` is the :dt:`unified type` of
00716|   the :t:`[break type]s` of all :t:`[break expression]s` and the :t:`type` of the
00717|   :t:`tail expression`.
00718| 
00719| * :dp:`fls_97v4fnekrRXI`
00720|   Otherwise, if the :t:`block expression` contains at least one
00721|   :t:`break expression`, then the :t:`type` is the :t:`unified type` of the
00722|   :t:`[break type]s` of all :t:`[break expression]s`.
00723| 
00724| * :dp:`fls_ob76y2ymdd27`
00725|   Otherwise, if the :t:`block expression` has a :t:`tail expression`, then the
00726|   :t:`type` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`tail expression`.
00727| 
00728| * :dp:`fls_u0avbm147nyh`
00729|   Otherwise the :t:`type` is the :t:`unit type`.
00730| 
00731| :dp:`fls_y6c2p9k4m1ra`
00732| The :dt:`unit value` is the :t:`value` of a :t:`unit type`.
00733| 
00734| :dp:`fls_1hzup0sf8l7l`
00735| The :t:`value` of a :t:`block expression` is determined as follows:
00736| 
00737| * :dp:`fls_kKZPKvJ902cw`
00738|   If the :t:`block expression` contains at least one :t:`break expression` and
00739|   a :t:`break expression` broke out the :t:`block expression`, then the
00740|   :t:`value` is the :t:`break value` of the :t:`break expression` that
00741|   broke out of the :t:`block expression`.
00742| 
00743| * :dp:`fls_9nmssjseq3jt`
00744|   Otherwise, if the :t:`block expression` has a :t:`tail expression`, then the
00745|   :t:`value` is the :t:`value` of the :t:`tail expression`.
00746| 
00747| * :dp:`fls_a3ulnvyc1ut`
00748|   Otherwise the :t:`value` is the :t:`unit value`.
00749| 
00750| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00751| 
00752| :dp:`fls_elcl73psruxw`
00753| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`block expression` proceeds as follows:
00754| 
00755| #. :dp:`fls_13b5n127rj92`
00756|    Each :t:`statement` is executed in declarative order.
00757| 
00758| #. :dp:`fls_nzdpw59plr2g`
00759|    The :t:`tail expression` is evaluated.
00760| 
00761| .. rubric:: Examples
00762| 
00763| .. code-block:: rust
00764| 
00765|    {
00766|        fn_call();
00767|        42
00768|    }
00769| 
00770| .. _fls_aadan19t5006:
00771| 
00772| Async Blocks
00773| ~~~~~~~~~~~~
00774| 
00775| .. rubric:: Syntax
00776| 
00777| .. syntax::
00778| 
00779|    AsyncBlockExpression ::=
00780|        $$async$$ $$move$$? BlockExpression
00781| 
00782| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00783| 
00784| :dp:`fls_hhidi5ukxo`
00785| An :dt:`async block expression` is a :t:`block expression` that is specified
00786| with :t:`keyword` ``async`` and encapsulates behavior which is executed in
00787| an asynchronous manner.
00788| 
00789| :dp:`fls_DnCb6ei84CeI`
00790| An :dt:`async block` is an :t:`async block expression`.
00791| 
00792| :dp:`fls_h36F1drHlHkH`
00793| A :dt:`control flow boundary` is a :t:`construct` that provides the target of
00794| a :t:`return expression` and bounds its control flow transfer.
00795| 
00796| :dp:`fls_sRfEgIkgKhlk`
00797| An :dt:`async control flow boundary` is a :t:`control flow boundary` introduced
00798| by an :t:`async block expression`, :dt:`async function`, or
00799| :t:`async closure expression`.
00800| 
00801| :dp:`fls_oisws5qykedi`
00802| An :t:`async block expression` denotes a new :t:`async control flow boundary`.
00803| 
00804| :dp:`fls_tzclkasinpoq`
00805| An :t:`async block expression` is subject to :t:`capturing`.
00806| 
00807| :dp:`fls_ncd0wkgtldem`
00808| The :t:`type` of an :t:`async block expression` is a unique anonymous :t:`type`
00809| that implement the :std:`core::future::Future` trait.
00810| 
00811| :dp:`fls_pvnofoomgwl5`
00812| The :t:`value` of an :t:`async block expression` is a :t:`value` of the
00813| :t:`async block expression`'s :t:`type`.
00814| 
00815| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00816| 
00817| :dp:`fls_9ghp5yet75y6`
00818| The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`async block expression` produces a :t:`value` of
00819| the :t:`type` of the :t:`async block expression` that :t:`captures <capturing>`
00820| the :t:`[capture target]s` of the :t:`async block expression`.
00821| 
00822| .. rubric:: Examples
00823| 
00824| .. code-block:: rust
00825| 
00826|    async {
00827|        42
00828|    }

00848| 
00849| :dp:`fls_veEGzEbpT4ny`

00959| Operator Expressions
00960| --------------------
00961| 
00962| .. rubric:: Syntax
00963| 
00964| .. syntax::
00965| 
00966|    OperatorExpression ::=
00967|        ArithmeticExpression
00968|      | AssignmentExpression
00969|      | BitExpression
00970|      | BorrowExpression
00971|      | ComparisonExpression
00972|      | CompoundAssignmentExpression
00973|      | DereferenceExpression
00974|      | ErrorPropagationExpression
00975|      | LazyBooleanExpression
00976|      | NegationExpression
00977|      | RawBorrowExpression
00978|      | TypeCastExpression
00979| 
00980| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00981| 
00982| :dp:`fls_ursc5ynymoy`
00983| An :dt:`operator expression` is an :t:`expression` that involves an operator.
00984| 
00985| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00986| 
00987| :dp:`fls_lSxXWxJn0vMO`
00988| An :t:`operator expression` that operates with :t:`[floating-point value]s` run as a :t:`constant expression` is allowed to yield different :t:`[value]s` compared to when run as a non-:t:`constant expression`.
